content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration in autonomous agents can be repurposed to actively chunk unstructured long-range reasoning inputs for LLMs, proactively fitting reasoning steps within the agent's fixed working memory capacity limit rather than retroactively trying to expand it. This turns a passive capacity constraint on LLM reasoning into an active exploration problem that rewards agents for discovering compact, memory-aligned reasoning schemas.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
